Granted Building Regulation Deviations:
For Waiving the Requirement for a Continuous Necessary Staircase to the Top Attic Floor (§ 32 Abs. 1 HBauO):
Condition: Suitable signage for the escape route in the attic (staircase offset) in both directions by signs according to BGV A8/DIN 4844.
For Waiving the Requirement for a Necessary Staircase for Apartment 4 (§ 33 Abs. 1 HBauO in conjunction with § 29 Abs. 4 HBauO):
Condition:
The building must be occupied by only one family.
The basement must be separated with fire-resistant walls and a connecting door as a fire-retardant, smoke-tight, and self-closing closure (T30-RS door).
The internal staircase must be constructed as a necessary staircase according to § 32 Abs. 4 HBauO.
For Waiving the Requirement for a Necessary Staircase for Apartments 8, 9, and 10 (§ 33 Abs. 1 HBauO in conjunction with § 29 Abs. 4 HBauO):
Condition:
The main staircase as the 1st escape route must also be constructed as a necessary staircase in the top floor according to § 33 HBauO. The staircase DIN 18065 must also be observed for this upper part.
The internal staircases must be constructed as necessary staircases according to § 32 Abs. 4 HBauO.
Additional Conditions:
Construction must not begin until the following inspection items have been approved in a supplementary notice:
Structural stability: The required proof according to § 14 of the BauVorlagenVerordnung (BauVorlVO) must be submitted for inspection.

Granted Exemptions:
Granted Exemptions:
1. Exception according to § 4 of the ordinance on the protection of tree stands and hedges in the Free and Hanseatic City of Hamburg (Tree Protection Ordinance) from September 17, 1948 in the current version.
Permission is granted, without prejudice to the rights of third parties, to fell the trees: Tree No. 6 (hornbeam) and Nos. 7/8 (hawthorn) according to the tree report (Appendix 4/8) and tree inventory plan (Appendix 4/4) between October 1 and February 28.
Permission is also granted to conduct unavoidable excavations in the root area of oak trees No. 4 and 5 according to the tree expert and to trim individual roots professionally.
Permission is granted to trim the entire crown of oak trees No. 4 and 5 by 1.50-2.00 m according to the tree care standards (ZTV - tree care point 3.3.1, edition 2017) to ensure construction clearance and avoid uncontrolled branch breakage.
Building regulations and requirements:
Building regulations and requirements:
1. The ground surface is set at +28.79 m above sea level (NN) in front of house 73 (reference point according to the development plan). This results in the building zero level of +28.97 m above sea level (NN) = upper edge of the finished floor of the ground floor (§ 2 paragraph 3 HBauO).
2. The start of construction must be notified to the building supervisory authority at least one week in advance (§ 72a paragraph 4 HBauO). Please use the form available on the online service "Notice of start, demolition and resumption of construction" on the website gateway.hamburg.de.
3. The builder must notify the building supervisory authority of the intended occupancy at least two weeks in advance. This does not apply to the demolition of facilities and the construction of non-structural advertising facilities (§ 77 paragraph 2 HBauO).
4. The outdoor areas must be created in accordance with § 2 of the development plan LM 20 and § 9 paragraph 1 HBauO until the final completion of the building. In case of failure, equivalent replacements must be planted. (see also points 6 to 10 in the decision)
5. Further information, leaflets and brochures for your construction can be found at the link: "http://www.hamburg.de/baugenehmigung/583468/start-merkblaetter.html".
6. Trees must be protected during construction with a fixed tree protection fence, securely anchored in the ground, at a distance of 4 m from the tree trunk, or similar measures.
7. Tree care measures must be carried out by a professional tree care company in accordance with the tree care standards (ZTV - current edition) while maintaining the natural growth form of the trees.
8. Excavations in the root area must be carried out using root-friendly methods (by hand or using a suction excavator). Damaged roots must be cut cleanly above the injury.
9. Due to the determined value of the tree stock according to the tree evaluation (Appendix 4/8), the nature conservation compensation requirement is set at 4 trees or € 4,000. This compensation will be partly in the form of planting 3 medium-crowned deciduous trees and a payment of € 1,000, as a new planting of 4 medium-crowned deciduous trees cannot be sustainably realized on the property.
10. The compensation plantings must be carried out in accordance with the planting quality specified in the tree inventory and evaluation.
11. A maintenance contract for the compensation plantings must be established with a qualified gardening company to ensure successful establishment of the plants for at least 2 years.
12. The compensation planting must be reported to the relevant authority after completion of the exterior facilities.
13. An offset payment of € 1,000 must be made for financing nature conservation and landscape maintenance measures.

Granted Exemptions:

2. Permission is granted, without prejudice to the rights of third parties, to fell the trees: Tree No. 2 (magnolia), Nos. 3 and 4 (hornbeam), No. 5 (laburnum), Nos. 6, 7, and 8 (European beech), Nos. 9-12, 14, 15 and Nos. 21-26 (spruces), No. 13 (goat willow), Nos. 16 and 20 (Norway maple), No. 19 (walnut), and No. 27 (Norway maple), as well as hedge No. 1 (hornbeam) of approx. 39 linear meters and hedge No. 35 (privet) of approx. 3 linear meters according to the site plan (Appendix 33/55) and the tree inventory report (Appendix 33/13).
3. Permission is granted to perform a crown maintenance cut on trees No. 29 (goat willow) and No. 31 (Norway maple) according to the site plan (Appendix 33/55) in the fine and weak branch area according to the tree care standards (ZTV tree care point 3.2.2, edition 2017) and to remove deadwood according to the tree care standards (ZTV tree care point 3.2.4, edition 2017).
Building regulations and requirements:
Building regulations and requirements:
1. Trees must be protected during construction with a fixed tree protection fence, securely anchored in the ground, at a height of 2 meters and a distance of 4 meters from the tree trunk or similar measures to prevent compaction from driving and material storage.
2. Tree protection measures (detailed planning) and the accompanying ecological supervision must be reported to the aforementioned office for approval before construction begins.
3. Tree protection measures (detailed planning) must be carried out according to DIN 18920 and RAS LP 4 (see Chapter 4.1) in collaboration with the tree expert.
4. Due to the determined value of the tree stock according to the open space plan including compensation calculation (Appendix 33/55) and the compensation value determination (Appendix 33/14), the nature conservation compensation requirement is set at 36 trees or €36,000. This compensation will be partly in the form of planting 9 native small-crowned trees, approximately 107 linear meters of hedges, and a payment of €20,000.
5. The compensatory planting must be carried out according to the planting quality specified in the tree inventory and compensation calculation.
6. A maintenance contract for the compensatory plantings must be established with a qualified gardening company to ensure successful establishment of the plants for at least 2 years.
7. The compensatory planting must be reported to the relevant authority after completion of the exterior facilities.
8. An offset payment of €20,000 must be made for financing nature conservation and landscape maintenance measures.
9. Regarding the offset payment, a separate payment request will be issued by WBZ-Nature Conservation after the legally binding building permit is obtained or immediately after the approved felling measures are carried out.
10. The outdoor areas must be created in accordance with § 2 of the development plan LM 20 and § 9 paragraph 1 HBauO until the final completion of the building. In case of failure, equivalent replacements must be planted. (see also points 6 to 10 in the decision)

Granted Exemptions:

1. Tree No. 11 (weeping beech) will have a lateral crown reduction (by 2-3m up to a height of 6m) in the direction of the construction field according to template 1/65, subject to strict specifications, instructions, and on-site supervision by the certified tree expert.
2. Tree No. 11 will have root area work including construction work, path construction, and root protection measures according to template 1/65 and 1/63, subject to strict specifications, instructions, and on-site supervision by the certified tree expert.
3. In the area of Tree No. 11, a support-free shoring (without slope) will be constructed according to template 1/65, subject to specifications, instructions, and on-site supervision by the certified tree expert. The escape route of the root trench corresponds to the maximum approach to the tree (according to template 1/65).
4. All work near the tree must be continuously supervised by the certified tree expert. The tree protection plan and tree protection report (templates 1/65, 1/63 including tree protection plan and site plan 1/64) must be strictly followed and implemented.
5. Root area work is only to be carried out by a certified tree care company accompanied by the certified tree expert (minimum requirement for on-site executor: certified tree care and tree sanitation specialist). This also applies to approved crown pruning work (professional execution according to ZTV-Tree Care 2017).
6. All construction work on the property must comply with and implement the nature conservation requirements and conditions. Protected trees/shrubs near the construction site must be protected throughout the entire construction period according to DIN 18920, RAS-LP4, ZTV-Tree Care 2017, and nature conservation requirements. Planning and execution work must be aligned with tree protection. This applies, among others, to protected beech No. N11 (see templates).

Building regulations and requirements:

Granted Exemptions:

This decision includes:
Exception permit according to § 4 of the ordinance on the protection of tree stands and hedges in the Free and Hanseatic City of Hamburg (Tree Protection Ordinance) from September 17, 1948 in the current version.

It is permitted, without prejudice to the rights of third parties, to fell the trees: No. 4 (birch), No. 8 (fir), No. 11 (elm), No. 12 (fir), and No. 20 (sycamore maple) as per the tree report (Appendix 20/30) and tree inventory plan, between October 1 and February 28.
Building regulations and requirements:
Building regulations and requirements:
1. The building permit expires if construction has not begun within three years of issuance or if construction is interrupted for more than one year. It can be extended on written application for up to one year each time.
2. The approval does not replace the need to obtain other public law decisions.
3. The green entries in the templates must be observed.
4. Tree protection: Before beginning any work, the crown and root area of the remaining trees, including hedges on the property and neighboring properties, must be secured according to the tree expert's instructions (e.g., with a fixed tree protection fence anchored in the ground, 4 m distance from the trunk base or similar, root curtain, etc.) to prevent compaction from driving and material storage.
5. Tree protection measures (detailed planning) and the accompanying ecological supervision must be reported to the aforementioned office for approval before construction begins. Tree protection measures (detailed planning) must be planned tree-friendly in collaboration with the tree expert according to DIN 18920 and RAS LP 4 (see Chapter 4.1).
6. Compensation for felling measures/replacement planting/replacement payment: Due to the determined value of the tree stock according to the tree evaluation (Appendix 20/30), the nature conservation replacement requirement is set at 12 trees or €12,000. This replacement planting or amount is set partly as a replacement planting of 9 native small to medium-crowned trees and partly as a replacement payment of €3,000, as it is not sustainably feasible to replant 12 small to medium-crowned trees on the property. Similarly, the permanent development ability of the trees according to their habitus cannot be ensured.
7. Replacement plantings must be implemented as suggested for replacement planting (Appendix 20/30) in planting species, scope, and locations, with a planting quality of high trunk, 3 times transplanted nursery stock, and a trunk circumference of at least 18-20 cm (§ 36 HmbVwVfG).
8. Execution of planting, including the necessary construction measures (e.g., preparation of planting pits 12 m²), must be carried out by a qualified gardening company.
9. Replacement plantings must be permanently maintained, and equivalent replacements planted in case of failure. Planting must be completed in the planting season after construction (by April 30, 2024).
10. To ensure the establishment of the replacement measures, qualified completion and development care for at least 2 years by a qualified gardening company is required.
11. Replacement planting must be reported to the aforementioned office after the exterior facilities are completed.
12. A replacement payment of €3,000 must be made to finance nature conservation and landscape maintenance measures (§ 36 HmbVwVfG) according to the replacement need calculation (Appendix 20/30). A separate payment request will be issued by WBZ-Nature Conservation after the legally binding building permit or immediately after the approved felling measures are carried out.

Granted Exemptions:
Granted Exemptions:
1. Exception permit according to § 6 Hamburgische Baumschutzverordnung (BaumschutzVO) in the current versions is granted, allowing the following between October 1 and February 28:
   - The removal of trees No. 3 (Blue Atlas Cedar), 4 (Sand Birch), 4a (False Cypress), 5 (Lilac), 6 (False Cypress), 9 (False Cypress), 11 (False Cypress), 12 (Bird Cherry), 13 (Sand Birch), 14 (Sand Birch), 16 (Sand Birch), 17 (Goat Willow), 18 (Goat Willow), 19 (Sand Birch), 20 (Sand Birch), 22 (False Cypress), 28 (Silver Fir), and 43 (Norway Spruce) as per the attachment 9/19.
   - The professional shortening of overhanging branches for trees No. 62, 67, and 68 to create light space profiles according to ZTV Tree Care standards (Attachment 9/19).
   - Execution of approved works in the protected crown and root areas following tree protection regulations under the supervision of a certified tree expert.

2. Other protected plants must be preserved permanently and protected according to DIN 18920 or nature conservation requirements throughout the construction period.

3. Replacement planting and greening measures must be implemented as per the attached plans (Attachment 9/19):
   - Planting of 5 medium to large-crowned trees (one each of Hornbeam, European Beech, Field Maple, Swedish Whitebeam, and Wych Elm), quality 3 x transplanted, trunk circumference 18-20 cm.
   - Planting of hedges using native species (Hornbeam and European Beech) along a total length of 260 linear meters, with 3 plants per meter, minimum quality 3x transplanted, height 125-150 cm.
   - Plants in protected root areas must be planted bare-rooted and hand-dug to avoid root damage.
   - All planting must be carried out by a qualified gardening company under the supervision of a landscape architect.
   - The plantings must be completed by April 30 in the first planting season following construction. They must be permanently maintained, and any failed plants must be replaced with equivalent species. The obligation extends to successors in title.
   - A qualified completion and development care of at least 2 years by a qualified gardening company is required to ensure the establishment of the replacement measures.
   - The responsible authority must be notified in writing with proof of planting completion.

4. The following specific tree protection measures must be observed (Attachment 9/17, 9/18, 9/19):
   - Protection of trees No. 62, 67, and 68 by tree-friendly construction methods and setting up a tree protection fence before starting work.
   - Continuous supervision of all planning and construction activities near trees by a certified tree expert.
   - Any necessary planning adjustments to protect trees must be made following the tree expert's guidance.
   - A tree protection fence must be set up before starting work and maintained throughout the construction period.
   - Any necessary construction in the root area must be done carefully by hand under the supervision of a tree expert.
   - Approved crown care measures must be performed by a certified tree care company.
Building regulations and requirements:
Building regulations and requirements:
1. The building permit expires if construction has not begun within three years of issuance or if construction is interrupted for more than one year. It can be extended on written application for up to one year each time.
2. The approval does not replace the need to obtain other public law decisions.
3. The green entries in the templates must be observed.
4. Species protection regulations must be observed. Before starting work, an independent biologist must check the area for protected species within 5 days. If nesting or presence of bats or squirrels is detected, felling must be postponed until after breeding and rearing.
5. A brief report outlining avoidance and mitigation measures must be submitted to the Nature Conservation Department (BUKEA). In case of incidents, BUKEA must be informed immediately.
6. The Nature Conservation Department must be informed of the appointment of the environmental construction supervision.

Granted Exemptions:
Granted Exemptions:
1. Exception permit according to § 4 of the ordinance on the protection of tree stands and hedges in the Free and Hanseatic City of Hamburg (Tree Protection Ordinance) in the current version for the felling and clearing of a birch (7, Ø approx. 63 cm).
   - The measure is carried out due to construction or condition requirements to clear the construction environment.
   - According to § 39 Federal Nature Conservation Act (BNatSchG) Abs. 5 Satz 2, the felling of the tree must be carried out between October 1 and February 28.
   - Interventions in the root area of protected trees for creating access roads and parking spaces on non-buildable areas in the front yard are not permissible.
Building regulations and requirements:
Building regulations and requirements:
1. The building permit expires if construction has not begun within three years of issuance or if construction is interrupted for more than one year. It can be extended on written application for up to one year each time.
2. The approval does not replace the need to obtain other public law decisions.
3. The green entries in the templates must be observed.

Specific conditions:
1. Following Can provisions are granted according to § 23 Abs. 5 BauNVO 1962:
   - For the southern terraces on non-buildable areas (§ 23 Abs. 5 BauNVO 1962 in connection with Bebauungsplan Rissen 8)
   - For the northern terraces on non-buildable areas (§ 23 Abs. 5 BauNVO 1962 in connection with Bebauungsplan Rissen 8)
2. Following nature conservation exemption is granted according to § 67 Abs. 1 BNatSchG:
   - For the implementation of the above measures from March 1 to September 30.

Reasoning:
- According to § 67 Abs. 1 Satz 1 Nummer 2 BNatSchG, an exemption can be granted if compliance with the summer felling ban would result in unreasonable hardship and if the deviation is compatible with the interests of nature conservation and landscape management. The felling of the birch is necessary for the construction project.
- The exemption is compatible with the interests of nature conservation and landscape management under the following conditions:

Resolutive conditions:
1. The permit becomes invalid if:
   - Current nesting or roosting sites of birds or mammals are found in the vegetation or nearby affected area or if they are discovered during the authorized measures. Work on the tree/vegetation must then be stopped, ensuring safety, and only resumed after consultation and approval from the responsible office (§ 39 and § 44 BNatSchG). If protected species are found, the measure must be postponed until the end of the breeding and rearing season, or an alternative must be coordinated with the specialist department. Exception permits in this case fall under the jurisdiction of the Authority for Environment, Climate, Energy and Agriculture (BUKEA - Neuenfelder Straße 19, 21109 Hamburg).

Suspensive condition:
1. The permit may only be used after:
   - A qualified expert (Dipl.-Biologe:in) has determined that no wild animals of particularly or strictly protected species and European bird species will be injured, killed, or have their developmental forms, reproductive or resting sites removed, damaged, or disturbed by the felling/clearing of the tree/vegetation structures (§ 44 Abs.1 Nr.1 - 3 BNatSchG). The responsible office must be informed of the assessment before felling/clearing for verification. Measures must be carried out within 5 days of the assessment.

2. The planned replacement planting must be submitted to the responsible office for review and coordination in a detailed and qualified planting plan created by a professional firm (landscape architect, planner, etc.) with details on species, quality, and quantity in a scale of at least M 1:200 before demolition of the existing building and before construction begins.

3. Tree protection measures according to the applicable tree protection regulations, DIN 18 920 with the RAS-LP 4, the Hamburg Tree Protection Ordinance, and the ZTV-Tree Care (2017 edition) must be implemented before the demolition of the existing building and the start of construction.

Granted Exemptions:
Granted Exemptions:
1. Exception permit according to § 4 of the ordinance on the protection of tree stands and hedges in the Free and Hanseatic City of Hamburg (Tree Protection Ordinance) for the felling of trees:
   - Tree No. 6 (Hornbeam) and Nos. 7/8 (Hawthorn) as per tree report (Attachment 4/8) and tree inventory plan (Attachment 4/4).

2. Permission to conduct unavoidable excavations in the root area of oak trees No. 4 and 5, and to properly shorten individual roots as per the arborist's instructions.
   - Permission to shorten the crown of oak trees No. 4 and 5 by 1.50-2.00 m according to ZTV Tree Care (2017 edition) to ensure construction clearance and prevent uncontrolled branch breakage.

Tree protection measures:
   - Protect the crown and root area of trees Nos. 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 9, and 10 (oaks and hawthorn) as per the tree inventory plan (Attachment 4/4) and tree report (Attachment 4/8) using measures such as a fixed tree protection fence anchored in the ground, with a distance of 4 m from the trunk base, or similar methods, to prevent soil compaction from driving and material storage.

Compensation:
   - Due to the determined value of the tree stock according to the tree evaluation (Attachment 4/8), the required nature conservation compensation is set at 4 trees or €4,000.
   - The compensation includes the planting of 3 medium-crowned native deciduous trees and a payment of €1,000, as planting 4 medium-crowned trees sustainably on the property is not feasible. 

Replacement planting:
   - The replacement planting must be implemented according to the tree inventory and evaluation, and the proposed replacement planting.
   - On the property, 3 medium-crowned trees must be planted according to the replacement planting proposal. Quality: high trunk, 3 times transplanted nursery stock, trunk circumference at least 18-20 cm.
   - The planting and required construction measures (e.g., preparation of planting pits 12 m²) must be carried out by a qualified gardening company.
   - The replacement planting must be permanently maintained, and equivalent replacements planted in case of failure. Planting must be completed in the planting season after construction (by April 30, 2022).
   - Qualified completion and development care for at least 2 years by a qualified gardening company is required to ensure the establishment of the replacement measures.
   - The responsible office must be notified of the replacement planting after the exterior facilities are completed.
   - A compensation payment of €1,000 must be made for financing nature conservation and landscape maintenance measures.
   - A separate payment request will be issued by WBZ-Nature Conservation after the legally binding building permit or immediately after the approved felling measures are carried out.

Specific conditions:
1. The following building code deviation is permitted according to § 69 HBauO:
   - Deviation from § 6 HBauO for overlapping distance areas by the West balcony of House 01 with the East facade of House 02.

Reason:
   - There are no neighborly or fire protection concerns, so the requirements for granting this deviation according to § 69 HBauO are met.

Discretion:
   - According to § 23 (3) BauNVO 1968, discretion is exercised for exceeding the building boundary by 1.50 m with 4 balconies, each 3.50 m wide, to the north and south, and by 1.50 m with 1 balcony, 4.50 m wide, to the west. The balconies are on the 1st floor, protrude 1.50 m, and occupy less than 1/3 of the facade width. The additional building impact is considered minor and acceptable. 
   - This discretion is exercised accordingly.

Suspensive condition:
   - The permit may only be used after the submission of the declaration of encumbrance according to § 79 paragraph 1 HBauO for the accessibility of the property (§ 4 Abs. 1 HBauO).

These conditions do not extend the validity of the permit according to § 73 paragraph 1 HBauO.

2. Permit for construction site setup according to § 19 Absatz 1 HWG from January 22, 1974, in the current version for the use of public land for a construction site setup in the area of Falkenstein 11.

Condition:
- The construction site setup must be coordinated with the street maintenance/supervision on-site.
- Special use fees are charged according to the "Fee Ordinance for the Administration and Use of Public Roads, Green and Recreational Areas" from December 6, 1994, in the current version.
- The final and decisive use area of the construction site setup will be measured on-site by the supervising authority, and a separate cost determination notice will be sent to the builder.

3. Permit for construction site access according to § 19 Absatz 1 HWG from January 22, 1974, in the current version for the use of public land for a construction site access in the area of Falkenstein 11.

Condition:
- The construction site access must be coordinated with the street maintenance/supervision on-site.

4. Permits according to § 22 Absatz 1 HWG for altering or digging up public roads (excavation permits).

Condition:
- These permits are valid until the completion of the construction measure.
- Each excavation must be coordinated with the supervising authority.

5. Permit according to § 3 Abs. 1 of the ordinance for the protection of landscape parts in the districts of Altona-Südwest, Ottensen, Othmarschen, Klein Flottbek, Nienstedten, Dockenhuden, Blankenese, and Rissen from December 18, 19
Building regulations and requirements:
Building regulations and requirements:
1. The building permit expires if construction has not begun within three years of issuance or if construction is interrupted for more than one year. It can be extended on written application for up to one year each time.
2. The approval does not replace the need to obtain other public law decisions.

Conditions:
1. Pruning and care cuts as well as root area interventions on the specified trees must be done in accordance with the landscape protection ordinance and under the supervision of a qualified biologist.
2. Tree protection measures must be implemented before starting work, including securing the crown and root areas of the remaining trees on the property with a fixed tree protection fence, anchored in the ground, 2 m high, with a distance of 4 m from the trunk base.
3. Compensation for felling measures and replacement planting must include planting 9 native small-crowned trees on the property and a payment of €20,000 for the non-feasible planting of 36 trees.
4. Replacement planting must be permanently maintained, and equivalent replacements planted in case of failure. Planting must be completed by April 30, 2024, under the supervision of a qualified gardening company.

Building regulations and requirements:
Building regulations and requirements:
1. The building permit expires if construction has not begun within three years of issuance or if construction is interrupted for more than one year. It can be extended on written application for up to one year each time.
2. The approval does not replace the need to obtain other public law decisions.

Conditions:
1. On the mentioned property, there is an area no. 7620-101/00 (former gas station) classified as "completed (was not AvF)" registered in the soil condition register of the Authority for Environment and Energy (BUE). During construction measures and earthworks, additional costs for the disposal of excavated soil or construction pit water may arise.
2. The property is located in a project area of the Authority for Environment and Energy (BUE) (GasBW-000), which includes an area of the Elbmarsch, where organic soft layers (clay, mud, and peat) are present in the subsoil. Due to decomposition processes in these soils, soil gases (methane [CH4] and carbon dioxide [CO2]) can naturally form. These gases can rise to near-surface soil layers, accumulate especially under sealed/built-up areas, and potentially penetrate buildings. However, according to current knowledge, these soft layers are not present in relevant thicknesses (> 2m) for the formation of soil gases in the area of the property. If soft layers with a thickness > 2m are encountered or known in the area of the construction project, structural safety measures to prevent gas accumulations and gas entries into the building must be provided, such as a gravel filter layer, gas-tight pipe penetrations, no enclosed spaces below the floor (§ 16 HBauO).
3. Topsoil must be preserved in a usable condition and protected from destruction or waste (§ 202 Baugesetzbuch). Excavated material, including topsoil, must be reused or recycled according to the applicable waste and soil protection regulations of the Federal Republic and the Free and Hanseatic City of Hamburg in accordance with § 6 Kreislaufwirtschaftsgesetz (KrWG). If reuse or recycling is not feasible according to legal requirements, the excavated material must be disposed of. For the reuse of excavated material suitable for creating a rootable soil layer or for placing in rootable soil layers due to its properties (humus content, absence of contaminants, structure), the requirements of § 12 Bundes-Bodenschutz- und Altlastenverordnung (BBodSchV) must be observed.
4. If irregularities in the subsoil (discoloration, odor, gas emissions) are detected during soil investigations or construction measures, the District Office Bergedorf, Technical Environmental Protection (Verbraucherschutz@bergedorf.hamburg.de) must be notified. Outside office hours, the damage management of the Authority for Environment and Energy, Tel.: 040/428 40-2300, must be informed (§ 1 Abs. 1 Hamburgisches Bodenschutzgesetz).
5. The start of construction must be reported to the building supervisory authority at least one week in advance (§ 72a Abs. 4 HBauO).
6. The intended use of the building must be reported to the building supervisory authority at least two weeks in advance (§ 77 Abs. 2 HBauO).

Granted Exemptions:
Granted Exemptions:
1. Planning exemption granted according to § 31 Absatz 2 BauGB:
   - For exceeding the allowable ground area of 300 m² by 25.32 m² to 325.32 m² for the left building (Immenschuur 16).

Reason:
The main areas comply with the allowable 200 m² according to the development plan. The exceedance of the ground area on one of the two properties by 25 m² due to ancillary facilities (underground garage and car lift) is urbanistically acceptable as it avoids ground-level parking spaces.

Condition:
The underground garage must be covered with at least 80 cm high substrate without creating a height jump in the terrain.

2. Planning exemption granted according to § 31 Absatz 1 BauGB:
   - For exceeding the building boundary by 4.00 m with the right building (Immenschuur 16a) towards the north.
   - For exceeding the building boundary by 3.00 m with the left building (Immenschuur 16) towards the north.
   - For exceeding the building boundary by 3.19 m with the left building (Immenschuur 16) towards the west.

3. Building code deviation permitted according to § 69 HBauO:
   - For allowing an ancillary building - car lift as garage access - in the front garden in a residential area (§ 9 Abs. 2 HBauO).

Reason:
The car lift avoids a significantly larger ramp system in favor of a small ancillary building resembling a garage.

Condition:
The roof must be greened.

   - Proof of thermal insulation and energy saving: The required documents according to § 16 BauVorlVO must be submitted for review.

These restrictions do not extend the validity of the permit according to § 73 Absatz 1 HBauO.

Granted Exemptions:
Granted Exemptions:
1. Exception permit according to § 4 of the ordinance on the protection of tree stands and hedges in the Free and Hanseatic City of Hamburg (Tree Protection Ordinance) from September 17, 1948, in the current version:
   - For felling the obstructive tree No. 1 (Spruce) and the obstructive hedges Nos. H4 and H5 (total of 39 meters) (see attachment 6 / 16).

2. Tree retention:
   - Other protected vegetation must be permanently retained and protected according to DIN 18920 or nature conservation requirements. Tree protection measures must be maintained throughout the construction period.

Condition:
   - Planting of one small to medium-crowned tree; planting in minimum quality 3 x v STU 18-20 cm using suitable native species. Each tree must have a rootable vegetation area of at least 12 square meters.
   - Planting of hedges using suitable native species, minimum length of 39 meters, with at least 3 plants per meter in minimum quality 3 x v m.B. 100-125 cm. A vegetation strip with a minimum width of 1 meter must be maintained.
   - The planting measures must be executed by a qualified gardening company in consultation with the landscape architect. They must be carried out in the planting season after construction completion, no later than April 30, and must be permanently maintained. In case of failure, equivalent replacements must be planted by April 30, 2025.
   - After fulfilling the requirements, the district office must be notified in writing with a planting plan and proof of the planting obligation.
   - To ensure the growth of replacement measures, a qualified gardening company must provide at least two years of development maintenance.

Special Tree Protection:
1. Tree protection requirements for further planning and execution must be strictly followed according to the tree expert opinion (attachment 6 / 16).
2. Before starting construction, the root area of the trees and hedges to be preserved must be protected according to the specifications of a tree expert (e.g., with a fixed tree protection fence). The protection must be carried out by a certified tree care company. If this is not possible, a trunk protection according to DIN 18920, section 4.6, must be installed, and the root area protected with steel plates on a gravel bed with a fleece underlay according to DIN 18920, section 4.12. The tree protection fence installation must be reported to the responsible office.

Tree Protection Site Management:
- All planning and construction processes in the tree environment must be checked, approved, and supervised by a tree expert. This applies to sealing and unsealing work, building construction, and outdoor facilities construction. Tree-friendly planning adjustments must be developed as needed.
- The tree protection site management must be named to the district office in advance. The tree expert must certify the proper implementation of measures monthly and after all work is completed, with the reports sent directly to the mentioned office.

Species Protection:
- Compliance with species protection regulations must be ensured. It is forbidden to catch, injure, kill, or destroy the habitats of protected species according to § 44 BNatSchG. Before starting work, the entire area must be thoroughly inspected for relevant structures, and the absence of prohibited conditions must be documented. For exceptions, the Authority for Environment, Climate, Energy, and Agriculture, N3 Species and Biotope Protection, must be contacted.

2. The approval does not replace the need to obtain other public law decisions.
